sourceMovie.title: How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days (2003) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hitch
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days clicked for you, Hitch (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days?
faq.answer: Try Hitch, When Harry Met Sally..., Annie Hall—each shares DNA with How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days?
faq.answer: How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days (2003) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
